name: tu_x60_c_no_tswrite
drl: rule RJ when $a : E1(tag == "z") $b : E0(this before[0ms,50ms] $a) then end
_finding: GRADUATED at D-182 (the D-181 landing — the lingering-del fix is IN; engine==oracle): D-181 discriminator: re-enter WITHOUT the ts write. Oracle 3x [RJ(59,38)]; engine [] — the TS WRITE IS NOT LOAD-BEARING (pure exit/re-enter suffices). PASSES under the fix. MECHANISM (D-181, trace-pinned — ⚖ IDENTITY-MODEL LAW instance #3): the exit's s0-del, staged on the then-UNLINKED path, LINGERS (nothing drains unlinked staging); at the linking right's flush the temporal node leaves the pre-tail re-entry INS visible (temporal joins stash no lefts) while the dstash hides the pre-tail DEL — the D-171 exemption's 'fresh same-fact ins' predicate reads only post-snapshot ins and misses the earlier-action/earlier-epoch re-entry — so the pair derives at the flush and the stale del drains at the pop, killing it BY VALUE. FIX (validated-then-reverted, D-181 verbatim): widen the exemption's scan to ALL pending s0-ins at TEMPORAL nodes (engine.rs ~5807 scan_n; plain joins keep the fresh-only scan — their pre-tail lefts are stashed so del-batching is unchanged).
